value.author: ci-security-telemetry-review-20260531
value.text: Final ci-security-telemetry-review: read-only pass completed. Findings: High - tracked sentry-telemetry gate calls ignored/local-only telemetry script, so required telemetry is not reproducible in a clean checkout; Medium - release gates can skip Sentry when secrets are absent and allow nonzero fatal/error thresholds with telemetry off/best-effort; Medium - workflows restore shared ~/.cache/sentry-cli cache in PR-visible jobs, increasing observability data/credential-cache risk; Low - Sentry CLI fallback uses a non-package 'sentry' binary while devDependency exposes sentry-cli. Verified: node scripts/check-secrets.mjs passed, pnpm audit --audit-level moderate passed, local Sentry/telemetry gate passed with available local credentials. Unverified: clean CI required-telemetry execution and current GitHub Actions run state.

value.author: code-quality-performance-review-20260531
value.text: Final code-quality/performance review findings: Medium: src/cli/commands/extension/doctor.ts:166-172 parses colon-delimited collision warnings by fixed indexes, but command/parser names are free-form and may contain colons; warning producers in src/core/extensions/loader.ts:1907 and :1966 place command before layer/name fields, so a command like acme:sync yields Conflicting extensions: project instead of package names. Fix by parsing collision warning fields from the right or emitting structured collision diagnostics, and add colon-in-command regression coverage. Low: src/cli/commands/extension/doctor.ts:150-196 groups extension_command_override_handler_overlap with later-registration collisions even though loader emits only extension_command_override_handler_overlap:<command> at src/core/extensions/loader.ts:1921; docs/SDK.md:165 says collision warnings include package names, but this case cannot. Either enrich the overlap warning with handler/override extension names or use a separate remediation that does not promise names/later-wins semantics. Checks: git diff --check passed; pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --incremental false -p tsconfig.json passed; pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --incremental false -p tsconfig.packages.json passed; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extension-command.spec.ts tests/unit/health-command.spec.ts passed 90/90. Note: the test wrapper invoked build before tests.

value.author: sdk-package-review-20260531
value.text:
Final sdk-package-review findings (read-only):

1. HIGH - Default `pm package init` scaffold can produce a package that fails after install because its generated nested extension imports `@unbrained/pm-cli/sdk` at runtime while only declaring a package-root peerDependency. The scaffold writes `pm.extensions: ["extensions/<name>"]`, `peerDependencies: { "@unbrained/pm-cli": "*" }`, and `extensions/<name>/index.js` with a bare SDK import (src/cli/commands/extension/scaffold.ts:50-145). Install resolves/copies only the concrete extension directory (src/core/packages/manifest.ts:249-281; src/cli/commands/extension/install-sources.ts:378-445; src/cli/commands/extension.ts:1071-1084). Local installs do not install dependencies, and npm installs deps at package root before copying, so nested extensions can lose package-root node_modules. Recommended fix: make the generated package use a root extension (`pm.extensions: ["."]`) or make the generated command import-free like extension-only scaffold; add an end-to-end test for `pm package init ./pkg` -> `pm install ./pkg --project` -> generated command executes in an isolated project.

2. MEDIUM - Runtime contracts do not address nested package lifecycle subcommands even though docs tell agents to use contracts for exact flags. `pm contracts --command package --flags-only --json` works but only returns top-level lifecycle flags; `pm contracts --command 'package init' --flags-only --json`, `'package install'`, and `'package catalog'` return Unknown command. `pm package catalog --help --json` exposes subcommand-only `--fields`, so contracts and help diverge. Refs: docs/COMMANDS.md:3-9, src/sdk/cli-contracts/enum-contracts.ts:28-81, src/cli/commands/contracts.ts:1533-1564, src/cli/register-setup.ts:204-290. Recommended fix: add first-class contract keys for nested lifecycle commands or make `--command` resolve Commander paths and include subcommand flags.

3. LOW - Machine-readable help for package/extension subcommands falls back to root narrative. `pm package init --help --json`, `pm package install --help --json`, and `pm package catalog --help --json` report intent "Provides deterministic project management workflows..." and examples `["pm init"]`, which is actively misleading for package authors. Refs: src/cli/help-content.ts:101-173 and 643-659, src/cli/help-json-payload.ts:238-294. Recommended fix: add `HELP_BY_COMMAND_PATH` entries for package/extension subcommands or nearest-ancestor fallback plus regression coverage.

No file edits were made intentionally. Unexpected side effect observed: package inspection changed only `.agents/pm/extensions/.managed-extensions.json` updated_at from 2026-05-31T09:47:41.573Z to 2026-05-31T10:38:38.115Z; not reverted per instructions. I did not run scaffold/install dogfood because this sub-agent was instructed to stay read-only.

Key commands run: pm --version; node -v; pnpm -v; git status --short; pm package --help --json; pm package init/install/catalog --help --json; pm contracts --command package/extension/install --flags-only --json; pm contracts --command 'package init'/'package install'/'package catalog' --flags-only --json; pm contracts --runtime-only --availability-only --json; pm package explore/manage/doctor/catalog --project --json; rg/sed/nl reads over docs/SDK.md, docs/EXTENSIONS.md, src/sdk*, src/cli/commands/extension*, src/core/packages*, contracts/help, and relevant tests.

value.author: codex-audit-20260531
value.text: Sub-agent findings integrated: CI/security review flagged private telemetry gate coupling and Sentry cache/fallback concerns; code-quality review found the new collision parser used fixed colon indexes and over-promised package names for command override/handler overlap warnings. Both are being treated as actionable fixes before PR.

value.author: codex-audit-20260531
value.text: Cleanup: agent-UX sub-agent accidentally created placeholder items pm-crpv, pm-lql3, and pm-11ag while probing command behavior. Closed all three through pm as invalid accidental sub-agent probe items; canonical audit item remains pm-kd9n.

value.author: agent-ux-contract-review-20260531
value.text: Final agent-ux-contract-review: read-only review completed aside from requested pm comments. Side effect: typo probes for create unexpectedly created pm-crpv/pm-lql3/pm-11ag because bootstrap typo-normalization silently rewrites misspelled mutating flags; lead cleanup closed them as invalid. Findings: High - src/cli/bootstrap-args.ts:432-480,502-528,679-735 plus tests/integration/help-runtime.spec.ts:218-228 silently autocorrect create flag typos such as --titel/--titl to --title and writes items with no warning; make typo rewrites advisory/visible for mutating commands, or require explicit retry/acceptance and include normalization_events in output/history. High - src/cli/commands/contracts.ts:318-359,1533-1564,1648-1754 and src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts:1122-1230 advertise action command_path values like package install/extension doctor but pm contracts --command "package install" --flags-only --json rejects them; accept advertised command paths/action names and return subcommand-specific flags. Medium - pm contracts --command calendar --availability-only --runtime-only --json exits 2 instead of a nonblocking availability=false optional-package record; synthesize package availability with install_command for availability-only. Medium - pm calendar --help text emits full root help before unknown-command guidance; intercept unknown help paths before commander writes root help. Medium - pm get default is deep/unbounded and duplicates linked metadata (src/cli/commands/get.ts:82-99,225-239; src/cli/register-list-query.ts:277-281); default to brief/standard and require --full for comments/body/deep links. Medium - invalid-value retry examples can rewrite the wrong flag because provided fields are sorted (src/cli/argv-utils.ts:17-26, src/cli/error-guidance.ts:285-299); pm list --sort upd --limit 1 suggests --limit priority. Commands sampled include context/list/get/search/health/package/extension help, contracts flags/schema/availability, optional command help, and invalid option/value paths.

message: Link PR review fixes and changelog check

value.author: codex-audit-20260531
value.text: PR review response: addressed Gemini and Sourcery suggestions by table-driving core command flag contract lookup, centralizing registration collision warning parsing, adding y-to-ies list alias candidate handling with coverage, defensively formatting typo trace events, and allowing telemetry query commands to execute directly unless they are .sh scripts. Addressed CodeRabbit valid findings by switching Sentry fallback to the modern sentry issue list executable and canceling accidental probe items so changelog generation ignores invalid tracker noise. Skipped CodeRabbit's .agents/pm removal suggestion because these tracker files were produced through pm commands, not direct edits, and are required project-management evidence for this audit.
